name: Apple and Butternut Squash Soup
recipeIngredient: 2 and a half cups chicken broth
3 pounds butternut squash, peeled, seeded, and cubed (8 cups)
21 ounces Granny Smith apples, peeled, cored, and chopped (3 cups)
2 and a half cups chicken broth
1 tablespoon fresh parsley, chopped, or 1 tablespoon fresh cilantro, chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large heavy pot, melt butter over medium heat.
2. Add onions and sauté until translucent, about 5 minutes.
3. Add curry powder and chili powder. Mix well.
4. Cook, stirring occasionally, for 5 minutes.
5. Stir in 2 and a half cups chicken broth, squash, apples, and salt and pepper to taste.
6. Bring to a boil. Reduce heat.
7. Cover and simmer until squash is very tender, about 30-45 minutes, stirring occasionally.
8. Strain soup, reserving liquid.
9. Puree vegetables in several batches.
10. In the same pot, combine vegetable puree, reserved cooking liquid, cream, and 2 and a half cups chicken broth.
11. Bring just to simmering.
12. Ladle soup into warm bowls.
13. Garnish with parsley.
